title: Nunjucks templates for agent prompt composition
narrative: Agent prompts are compiled at dispatch time from YAML specs + Nunjucks templates + resolved input data. The rendered markdown exists only transiently (temp file passed to pi --append-system-prompt, then deleted). Nunjucks was chosen over string interpolation to support structured prompt authoring with conditionals ({% if %}), loops ({% for %}), template inheritance ({% extends %}), and filters. These constructs enable agent prompts that adapt to input shape. Three-tier resolution (project > user > builtin) enables user customization without forking package code. The tradeoff is that Nunjucks silently renders undefined variables as empty strings, which motivated the template-validation.ts module (commit c40f432). The specific choice of Nunjucks over other template engines (Handlebars, EJS) is not documented; it may have been an initial implementation decision. Confidence: MEDIUM (architecture documented; engine choice inferred).
